steps.action: Boot QEMU with 9p share for fonts
steps.observed: Boot sequence completed: 4 SMP cores, 5 virtio devices, fonts loaded from 9p

steps.expected: Display shows chrome bar, text content area, cursor
steps.observed: Screenshot shows chrome bar with document icon + 'Text' label + clock (10:33:12), dark content area with cursor visible

steps.expected: Text appears rendered with proper glyphs
steps.observed: Text 'helloworld' rendered in monospace font with clear, properly shaped glyphs. Subpixel rendering visible, stem darkening correct.

steps.expected: Glyphs are readable and properly positioned
steps.observed: All characters clearly readable at expected size and position. No rendering artifacts, no missing characters, no misalignment.

steps.expected: Cursor visible at initial position (top-left of content area)
steps.observed: Thin vertical cursor line visible at position (0,0) of content area in boot screenshot

steps.expected: Cursor moves to end of typed text
steps.observed: Cursor visible at end of 'helloworld' text, positioned correctly after the 'd' character

steps.action: Check serial output for pipeline initialization
steps.expected: No panics or crashes. Full pipeline starts.
steps.observed: Serial log confirms: scene graph shared memory allocated, compositor started with damage tracking, core entered event loop, display pipeline running. No panics.
Screenshot shows chrome bar, text content, cursor — full Core→SceneGraph→Compositor→GPU pipeline functioning.
grep for panic/crash/error returned no matches

steps.action: Check serial log for SVG loading
steps.observed: Serial shows: 'loading doc-icon.svg' (133 bytes), 'loading img-icon.svg' (173 bytes), 'parsing SVG doc icon', 'SVG icon rasterized'

steps.expected: Document icon visible in chrome bar
steps.observed: Small document icon visible in the chrome bar to the left of the 'Text' label. Icon appears as a properly rendered document glyph.

frictions.description: QEMU boot requires 9p filesystem share (-fsdev + virtio-9p-device) for font loading. Without it, compositor reports 'no font data' and display is blank. The task's QEMU command template was missing these flags.
frictions.resolution: Referenced system/test-qemu.sh to discover the required 9p share flags and added them to the QEMU command.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. QEMU boots successfully with full display pipeline (Core→SceneGraph→Compositor→GPU). Chrome bar shows SVG document icon, 'Text' label, and live clock. Text rendering works correctly with monospace font. Cursor visible and tracks input position. No panics or crashes in serial output. Required 9p share for font loading (not in original QEMU command template).
